req.title: Bulk import employees — variant 4
req.statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers bulk CSV import with per-row validation, partial commit (valid rows only), error reporting, rate limiting, and two explicit error pathways (415 for non-CSV, 422 for missing required headers). I designed tests using EP (happy-path valid+invalid mix), NEG (wrong MIME type, missing header), BVA (single-row CSV at the boundary of a minimal valid payload), DT (mixed valid/invalid rows triggering conditional commit logic), and STC (rate-limit state: allowed → throttled after N requests). Each test is self-contained via fixtures that authenticate an hr-admin session, upload a file, and clean up imported rows.

tests.name: test_bulk_import_mixed_rows_commits_valid_and_reports_errors
preconditions: CSV contains 3 valid rows and 2 invalid rows (missing required field)
actions: Construct a CSV with headers: employee_id,first_name,last_name,email
Add 3 fully populated valid rows
Add 2 rows with empty email (invalid)
POST multipart/form-data to /employees/import with the CSV file
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 207 Multi-Status (or 200 with partial result body)
Response body lists 3 imported rows
Response body lists 2 error entries with row numbers and error descriptions
Database contains exactly the 3 valid employees after import
trace: R-WEB-100: error report generated per row
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io

def test_bulk_import_mixed_rows_commits_valid_and_reports_errors(
    hr_admin_session,   # <fixture>: authenticated requests.Session
    base_url,           # <fixture>: str e.g. 'http://hr-app'
    cleanup_employees,  # <fixture>: deletes employees created during test
):
    csv_data = (
        "employee_id,first_name,last_name,email\n"
        "E001,Alice,Smith,alice@example.com\n"
        "E002,Bob,Jones,bob@example.com\n"
        "E003,Carol,White,carol@example.com\n"
        "E004,Dan,Brown,\n"
        "E005,Eve,Black,\n"
    )
    files = {"file": ("employees.csv", io.BytesIO(csv_data.encode()), "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(f"{base_url}/employees/import", files=files)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 207)
    body = resp.json()
    imported = body.get("imported", [])
    errors = body.get("errors", [])

    assert len(imported) == 3, f"Expected 3 imports, got {imported}"
    assert len(errors) == 2, f"Expected 2 errors, got {errors}"
    imported_ids = {r["employee_id"] for r in imported}
    assert imported_ids == {"E001", "E002", "E003"}
    error_rows = {e["row"] for e in errors}
    assert error_rows == {5, 6} or error_rows == {4, 5}
    cleanup_employees(["E001", "E002", "E003"])

tests.name: test_bulk_import_non_csv_file_returns_415
actions: Construct a plain JSON payload disguised as an import file
POST multipart/form-data to /employees/import with Content-Type application/json
Capture HTTP response status
expected: HTTP 415 Unsupported Media Type
No employees are created in the database

tests.name: test_bulk_import_missing_required_header_returns_422
preconditions: CSV omits the required 'email' column header
actions: Construct a CSV with headers: employee_id,first_name,last_name (email missing)
Add one data row
POST multipart/form-data to /employees/import with the CSV file
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity
Response body identifies the missing required column
No employees are created in the database
trace: R-WEB-100: 422 when header missing required columns

tests.name: test_bulk_import_single_row_csv_boundary
preconditions: CSV contains exactly 1 valid data row (minimum non-empty input)
actions: Construct a CSV with all required headers and exactly 1 valid data row
POST multipart/form-data to /employees/import
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 200 or 207
Response reports exactly 1 imported row
Response reports 0 errors
Employee record exists in the database
trace: BVA: minimum boundary of 1 data row
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io

def test_bulk_import_single_row_csv_boundary(
    hr_admin_session,   # <fixture>: authenticated requests.Session
    base_url,           # <fixture>: str
    cleanup_employees,  # <fixture>: callable(list[str])
):
    csv_data = (
        "employee_id,first_name,last_name,email\n"
        "E020,Minnie,Bound,minnie@example.com\n"
    )
    files = {"file": ("employees.csv", io.BytesIO(csv_data.encode()), "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(f"{base_url}/employees/import", files=files)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 207)
    body = resp.json()
    assert len(body.get("imported", [])) == 1
    assert len(body.get("errors", [])) == 0
    cleanup_employees(["E020"])

tests.name: test_bulk_import_rate_limit_triggers_after_threshold
preconditions: Rate-limit threshold N is known (e.g. 5 requests per minute, read from config)
User has not made any import requests in current rate-limit window
actions: Send N valid CSV import requests sequentially as the same hr-admin user
Assert each of the first N responses is 200/207
Send one additional (N+1) request
Capture response status of the N+1 request
expected: First N requests succeed with HTTP 200 or 207
The N+1 request is rejected with HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
HTTP 429 response includes Retry-After header
trace: R-WEB-100: operation must be rate-limited per user
STC: state transition from allowed -> throttled after N requests

tests.name: test_bulk_import_all_invalid_rows_commits_nothing
preconditions: CSV has correct headers but every data row fails validation
actions: Construct a CSV where all rows have empty required fields
POST multipart/form-data to /employees/import
Capture HTTP response status and body
Query database for any newly created employees from this batch
expected: HTTP 200 or 207
Response reports 0 imported rows
Response reports errors for every row submitted
No new employee records in the database
trace: R-WEB-100: commit only rows that pass validation
R-WEB-100: error report generated per row
DT: decision branch where NO rows satisfy validation predicate
tests.executable:
import pytest
import io

def test_bulk_import_all_invalid_rows_commits_nothing(
    hr_admin_session,  # <fixture>: authenticated requests.Session
    base_url,          # <fixture>: str
):
    csv_data = (
        "employee_id,first_name,last_name,email\n"
        ",,,\n"
        ",,,\n"
        ",,,\n"
    )
    files = {"file": ("employees.csv", io.BytesIO(csv_data.encode()), "text/csv")}
    resp = hr_admin_session.post(f"{base_url}/employees/import", files=files)

    assert resp.status_code in (200, 207)
    body = resp.json()
    assert len(body.get("imported", [])) == 0, "No rows should be imported"
    assert len(body.get("errors", [])) == 3, "All 3 rows should report errors"
